# Figure description: Схема_изоглубин_по_Т3.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Общие_данные_для_всех_листов/Схема_изоглубин_по_Т3.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is an **isodepth (isoglubina) map** — a contour map showing the depth to a key seismic reflector horizon. In Russian geological practice, such maps ("схема изоглубин") depict the subsurface configuration of a stratigraphic or structural horizon by connecting points of equal depth, analogous to a structure contour map. It is drawn at **1:500,000 scale** and constitutes **Drawing (Чертёж) No. 108** of the report.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The map covers a large region in **northern Kazakhstan**, spanning multiple 1:200,000 map sheets explicitly listed in the header:

- **N-42:** sheets 126-BG, 128-G, 128-G (repeated), 129-BG, 137-BG, 138, 139, 140, 141
- **M-42:** sheets 3-BG, 4, III, 7-AB, 8-A, B, 9, 15, 18; VII(8П), VIII(3П), IX(3П), 51, 52, XV, 44-A, B

The subject is the **reference (опорный) seismic reflector horizon Ts (Тs)** — almost certainly the top of the **Владимировская (Vladimirskaya) suite** or an equivalent Paleozoic/Mesozoic basement marker. The figure relates to the **Тениzская (Teniz) depression/basin** (Тенизская впадина), which is explicitly named in the legend and in an annotation referencing the **Западно-Тенизская партия (West-Teniz Party)** report for 1978–84.

The mapped area is irregular in outline, assembled from multiple adjacent sheet tiles, with cross-hatched zones indicating areas of data absence or poor seismic quality.

---

## Key Content

### Isodepth Contours
- Contour lines (изоглубины) depict the depth to the Ts horizon across the study area.
- **Contour values** are labeled numerically on the map; visible values range approximately from **~500 m to >2000 m** (and possibly deeper in the darkest pink zones, likely >1500–2000 m based on the colour scale).
- The contours define a complex structural surface with multiple **local highs (anticlinal domes/closures)** visible as closed, roughly circular or oval contour loops — these are the most exploration-relevant features.

### Colour Fill (Intensity Scale — Шкала интенсивности изоглубин)
A colour scale on the right side grades from **white/uncoloured → light pink → medium pink → dark pink/salmon**, corresponding to increasing isodepth values (i.e., greater burial depth):
- **White/unshaded**: shallow depths (~0–500 m or areas at surface)
- **Light pink**: moderate depths (~500–1000 m)
- **Medium pink**: greater depths (~1000–1500 m)
- **Dark pink/salmon**: deepest zones (>1500–2000 m+)

The darkest pink concentrations appear in the **southwestern and south-central portions** of the map, indicating the deepest parts of the Teniz basin depocenter. Several isolated **anticlinal highs** appear as lighter (shallower) closed contour loops surrounded by deeper-coloured areas.

### Structural Features
- Multiple **anticlinal closures** are visible across the map as closed contour loops at shallower depths — these represent potential hydrocarbon or mineral traps.
- A prominent **NW–SE trending structural axis** appears to run through the central portion.
- **Cross-hatched zones** (hatched in two directions) indicate areas where seismic correlation was lost due to poor data quality, faulting, or surface outcrop of the Vladimirskaya suite.

### Legend Elements (Условные обозначения)
The legend identifies the following map symbols:
1. **Изоглубины** — isodepth contour lines
2. **Зоны потери корреляции отражённого вала** (zones of lost reflection correlation), predominantly caused by faulting or sharp thinning — shown as diagonal cross-hatching
3. **Граница Тенизской впадины** (Boundary of the Teniz depression) — shown as a bold irregular line
4. **Выходы на дневную поверхность отложений Владимировской свиты и более древних пород по сейсмическим данным ДГРЗ** (Surface outcrops of Vladimirskaya suite and older rocks per DGRZ seismic data)
5. **Выходы на дневную поверхность отложений Владимировской свиты по сейсмическим данным** (Vladimirskaya suite surface outcrops per seismic data)
6. **Выходы на дневную поверхность отложений Владимировской свиты и более древних пород по геологическим данным** (Surface outcrops per geological data)

### Inset Sheet Layout Diagram
A small **"Схема расположения листов"** (sheet layout index diagram) appears in the upper right, showing the arrangement of individual 1:200,000 map sheets assembled into this composite map, with Roman numerals labeling sheet positions.
